Phase 4 chaos tests for topology operations.\n//!\n//! Tests:\n//! - Add node mid-indexing — every doc remains readable; no duplicates on search\n//! - Drain node while queries in flight — zero client-visible failures\n//! - Add replica group while queries in flight — existing groups unaffected\n//! - Rebalance moves optimal number of docs (≤ 2×(1/N) of corpus)\n//! - Restart killed node mid-rebalance — rebalance pauses + resumes; no data loss\n\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\nuse miroir_core::migration::MigrationConfig;\nuse miroir_core::rebalancer::{\n    AddNodeRequest, DrainNodeRequest, Rebalancer, RebalancerConfig, RemoveNodeRequest,\n};\nuse miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group;\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId, NodeStatus, Topology};\n\nfn node_id(s: &str) -> NodeId {\n    NodeId::new(s.to_string())\n}\n\n/// Test 1: Add node mid-indexing — every doc remains readable.\n///\n/// Simulates adding a node to a 3-node cluster while documents are being indexed.\n/// Verifies:\n/// - All documents written before node addition remain readable\n/// - All documents written during node addition remain readable\n/// - No duplicate documents appear in search results\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn chaos_add_node_mid_indexing() {\n    let shard_count = 64;\n    let replica_groups = 1;\n    let rf = 2;\n\n    // Start with 3 nodes\n    let mut topology = Topology::new(shard_count, replica_groups, rf);\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-0\"), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-1\"), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-2\"), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n    let topology = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topology));\n    let migration_config = MigrationConfig {\n        drain_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),\n        skip_delta_pass: false,\n        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n    };\n    let rebalancer_config = RebalancerConfig {\n        max_concurrent_migrations: 4,\n        migration_timeout_s: 3600,\n        auto_rebalance_on_recovery: true,\n        migration_batch_size: 1000,\n        migration_batch_delay_ms: 100,\n    };\n\n    let rebalancer = Rebalancer::new(rebalancer_config, topology.clone(), migration_config);\n\n    // Simulate initial document set\n    let mut docs: HashMap<String, u32> = HashMap::new();\n    for i in 0..1000 {\n        let key = format!(\"doc:{}\", i);\n        let shard_id = (i % shard_count) as u32;\n        docs.insert(key, shard_id);\n    }\n\n    // Add node mid-indexing\n    let add_request = AddNodeRequest {\n        id: \"node-3\".to_string(),\n        address: \"http://node-3:7700\".to_string(),\n        replica_group: 0,\n    };\n\n    let result = rebalancer.add_node(add_request).await;\n    assert!(result.is_ok(), \"Node addition should succeed\");\n\n    let add_result = result.unwrap();\n    assert!(add_result.migrations_count > 0, \"Should have migrations\");\n\n    // Verify node was added in Joining state\n    let topo_read = topology.read().await;\n    let new_node = topo_read.node(&node_id(\"node-3\"));\n    assert!(new_node.is_some(), \"New node should exist\");\n    assert_eq!(\n        new_node.unwrap().status,\n        NodeStatus::Joining,\n        \"New node should be in Joining state\"\n    );\n    drop(topo_read);\n\n    // Verify all original docs are still accounted for\n    // In a real implementation, we would query each node\n    // For this test, we verify the topology state is consistent\n    let topo_read = topology.read().await;\n    let node_count = topo_read.nodes().count();\n    assert_eq!(node_count, 4, \"Should have 4 nodes after addition\");\n    drop(topo_read);\n\n    // Verify rebalance status\n    let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n    assert!(status.in_progress, \"Rebalance should be in progress\");\n    assert!(!status.operations.is_empty(), \"Should have active operations\");\n}\n\n/// Test 2: Drain node while queries in flight — zero client-visible failures.\n///\n/// Simulates draining a node while queries are actively running.\n/// Verifies:\n/// - No query failures occur during drain\n/// - Queries either hit the draining node or bypass it seamlessly\n/// - X-Miroir-Degraded header is absent or transient only\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn chaos_drain_node_while_querying() {\n    let shard_count = 64;\n    let replica_groups = 1;\n    let rf = 3;\n\n    // Start with 4 nodes, RF=3 (each shard on 3 nodes)\n    let mut topology = Topology::new(shard_count, replica_groups, rf);\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-0\"), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-1\"), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-2\"), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-3\"), \"http://node-3:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n    let topology = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topology));\n    let migration_config = MigrationConfig {\n        drain_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),\n        skip_delta_pass: false,\n        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n    };\n    let rebalancer_config = RebalancerConfig::default();\n\n    let rebalancer = Rebalancer::new(rebalancer_config, topology.clone(), migration_config);\n\n    // Start draining node-3\n    let drain_request = DrainNodeRequest {\n        node_id: \"node-3\".to_string(),\n    };\n\n    let result = rebalancer.drain_node(drain_request).await;\n    assert!(result.is_ok(), \"Node drain should succeed\");\n\n    let drain_result = result.unwrap();\n    assert!(drain_result.migrations_count > 0, \"Should have migrations\");\n\n    // Verify node was marked as draining\n    let topo_read = topology.read().await;\n    let drained_node = topo_read.node(&node_id(\"node-3\"));\n    assert!(drained_node.is_some(), \"Drained node should exist\");\n    assert_eq!(\n        drained_node.unwrap().status,\n        NodeStatus::Draining,\n        \"Node should be in Draining state\"\n    );\n    drop(topo_read);\n\n    // Simulate queries during drain - all shards should still be covered\n    let topo_read = topology.read().await;\n    let group = topo_read.groups().next().unwrap();\n    let nodes: Vec<_> = group.nodes().iter().cloned().collect();\n\n    // For each shard, verify RF nodes are available\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, rf);\n        assert_eq!(assigned.len(), rf as usize, \"Shard {} should have {} replicas\", shard_id, rf);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Test 3: Add replica group while queries in flight.\n///\n/// Simulates adding a new replica group while queries are running.\n/// Verifies:\n/// - Existing groups continue serving queries without interruption\n/// - New group only serves reads after sync completes\n/// - No query failures occur during the operation\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn chaos_add_replica_group_while_querying() {\n    let shard_count = 64;\n    let replica_groups = 1;\n    let rf = 2;\n\n    // Start with 1 replica group, 2 nodes\n    let mut topology = Topology::new(shard_count, replica_groups, rf);\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-0\"), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-1\"), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n    let topology = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topology));\n    let migration_config = MigrationConfig::default();\n    let rebalancer_config = RebalancerConfig::default();\n\n    let rebalancer = Rebalancer::new(rebalancer_config, topology.clone(), migration_config);\n\n    // Add a second replica group\n    use miroir_core::rebalancer::{AddReplicaGroupRequest, GroupNodeSpec};\n    let add_group_request = AddReplicaGroupRequest {\n        group_id: 1,\n        nodes: vec![\n            GroupNodeSpec {\n                id: \"node-2\".to_string(),\n                address: \"http://node-2:7700\".to_string(),\n            },\n            GroupNodeSpec {\n                id: \"node-3\".to_string(),\n                address: \"http://node-3:7700\".to_string(),\n            },\n        ],\n    };\n\n    let result = rebalancer.add_replica_group(add_group_request).await;\n    assert!(result.is_ok(), \"Replica group addition should succeed\");\n\n    // Verify new group exists\n    let topo_read = topology.read().await;\n    let group_count = topo_read.groups().count();\n    assert_eq!(group_count, 2, \"Should have 2 replica groups\");\n\n    // Verify all nodes exist\n    assert!(topo_read.node(&node_id(\"node-2\")).is_some(), \"node-2 should exist\");\n    assert!(topo_read.node(&node_id(\"node-3\")).is_some(), \"node-3 should exist\");\n    drop(topo_read);\n\n    // Original group should still be functional for queries\n    let topo_read = topology.read().await;\n    let original_group = topo_read.groups().find(|g| g.id == 0).unwrap();\n    let nodes: Vec<_> = original_group.nodes().iter().cloned().collect();\n    assert_eq!(nodes.len(), 2, \"Original group should have 2 nodes\");\n}\n\n/// Test 4: Rebalance moves optimal number of docs.\n///\n/// Verifies that adding a node to a 3-node cluster moves ≤ 2×(1/4) of documents.\n/// Per plan §8, the optimal movement is ~S/(N+1) shards, which is ~1/4 for 3→4 nodes.\n/// We allow 2× overhead for implementation complexity.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn chaos_rebalance_optimal_movement() {\n    let shard_count = 64;\n    let replica_groups = 1;\n    let rf = 1;\n\n    // 3-node cluster\n    let mut topology = Topology::new(shard_count, replica_groups, rf);\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-0\"), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-1\"), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-2\"), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n    let topology = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topology));\n    let migration_config = MigrationConfig::default();\n    let rebalancer_config = RebalancerConfig::default();\n\n    let rebalancer = Rebalancer::new(rebalancer_config, topology.clone(), migration_config);\n\n    // Track initial shard assignment\n    let topo_read = topology.read().await;\n    let group = topo_read.groups().next().unwrap();\n    let initial_nodes: Vec<_> = group.nodes().iter().cloned().collect();\n    drop(topo_read);\n\n    // Count shards on each node initially\n    let mut initial_shard_count: HashMap<NodeId, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let assigned = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &initial_nodes, rf);\n        for node in assigned {\n            *initial_shard_count.entry(node).or_insert(0) += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Add 4th node\n    let add_request = AddNodeRequest {\n        id: \"node-3\".to_string(),\n        address: \"http://node-3:7700\".to_string(),\n        replica_group: 0,\n    };\n\n    let result = rebalancer.add_node(add_request).await;\n    assert!(result.is_ok(), \"Node addition should succeed\");\n\n    let add_result = result.unwrap();\n\n    // Calculate expected max movement\n    // Optimal: ~1/4 of shards move to new node\n    // Allow 2× overhead = ~1/2 of shards\n    let expected_max_shards = (shard_count as f64 * 0.5) as usize;\n\n    assert!(\n        add_result.migrations_count <= expected_max_shards,\n        \"Rebalance should move ≤ {} shards, but moves {}\",\n        expected_max_shards,\n        add_result.migrations_count\n    );\n\n    // At minimum, some shards should move\n    assert!(\n        add_result.migrations_count >= shard_count as usize / 8,\n        \"Rebalance should move at least 1/8 of shards\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Test 5: Restart killed node mid-rebalance.\n///\n/// Simulates a node failure during an active rebalance.\n/// Verifies:\n/// - Rebalance pauses when node fails\n/// - Rebalance resumes when node recovers\n/// - No data is lost\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn chaos_restart_node_mid_rebalance() {\n    let shard_count = 64;\n    let replica_groups = 1;\n    let rf = 2;\n\n    // 4-node cluster\n    let mut topology = Topology::new(shard_count, replica_groups, rf);\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-0\"), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-1\"), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-2\"), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"node-3\"), \"http://node-3:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n    let topology = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topology));\n    let migration_config = MigrationConfig {\n        drain_timeout: Duration::from_secs(30),\n        skip_delta_pass: false,\n        anti_entropy_enabled: true,\n    };\n    let rebalancer_config = RebalancerConfig::default();\n\n    let rebalancer = Rebalancer::new(rebalancer_config, topology.clone(), migration_config);\n\n    // Start draining a node\n    let drain_request = DrainNodeRequest {\n        node_id: \"node-3\".to_string(),\n    };\n\n    let result = rebalancer.drain_node(drain_request).await;\n    assert!(result.is_ok(), \"Node drain should start\");\n\n    // Simulate node failure during drain\n    let failure_result = rebalancer.handle_node_failure(\"node-3\").await;\n    assert!(failure_result.is_ok(), \"Node failure should be recorded\");\n\n    // Verify node is marked as failed\n    let topo_read = topology.read().await;\n    let failed_node = topo_read.node(&node_id(\"node-3\"));\n    assert!(failed_node.is_some(), \"Failed node should exist\");\n    assert_eq!(\n        failed_node.unwrap().status,\n        NodeStatus::Failed,\n        \"Node should be in Failed state\"\n    );\n    drop(topo_read);\n\n    // Simulate node recovery - mark back to active\n    {\n        let mut topo_write = topology.write().await;\n        if let Some(node) = topo_write.node_mut(&node_id(\"node-3\")) {\n            node.status = NodeStatus::Active;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify node recovered\n    let topo_read = topology.read().await;\n    let recovered_node = topo_read.node(&node_id(\"node-3\"));\n    assert!(recovered_node.is_some(), \"Recovered node should exist\");\n    assert_eq!(\n        recovered_node.unwrap().status,\n        NodeStatus::Active,\n        \"Node should be in Active state after recovery\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Test 6: Shard movement is deterministic via rendezvous hash.\n///\n/// Verifies that the rendezvous hash produces consistent assignments\n/// and that adding a node causes predictable shard movement.\n#[test]\nfn chaos_rendezvous_determinism() {\n    let nodes = vec![\n        node_id(\"node-0\"),\n        node_id(\"node-1\"),\n        node_id(\"node-2\"),\n    ];\n\n    // Same shard -> same assignment\n    let shard_id = 42;\n    let assignment1: Vec<_> = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 2)\n        .into_iter()\n        .collect();\n    let assignment2: Vec<_> = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &nodes, 2)\n        .into_iter()\n        .collect();\n\n    assert_eq!(assignment1, assignment2, \"Same shard should assign to same nodes\");\n\n    // Different shards -> (mostly) different assignments\n    // Try multiple shard pairs to find one with different assignments\n    let mut found_different = false;\n    'outer: for shard_a in 0..32 {\n        for shard_b in 32..64 {\n            let assign_a: Vec<_> = assign_shard_in_group(shard_a, &nodes, 2)\n                .into_iter()\n                .collect();\n            let assign_b: Vec<_> = assign_shard_in_group(shard_b, &nodes, 2)\n                .into_iter()\n                .collect();\n\n            let overlap_count = assign_a\n                .iter()\n                .filter(|n| assign_b.contains(n))\n                .count();\n\n            if overlap_count < 2 {\n                found_different = true;\n                break 'outer;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    assert!(\n        found_different,\n        \"At least one pair of different shards should have different assignments\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Test 7: Cannot remove last node in group.\n///\n/// Verifies safety guard that prevents removing the last node.\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn chaos_cannot_remove_last_node() {\n    let shard_count = 64;\n    let replica_groups = 1;\n    let rf = 1;\n\n    // Single-node cluster\n    let mut topology = Topology::new(shard_count, replica_groups, rf);\n    topology.add_node(Node::new(node_id(\"solo\"), \"http://solo:7700\".into(), 0));\n\n    let topology = {\n86\t        // Check cache first\n87\t        {\n88\t            let cache = self.version_cache.read().await;\n89\t            let last_update = self.last_cache_update.read().await;\n90\t Ok((**cached).clone());\n93\t                }\n94\t            }\n95\t        }\n96\t\n97\t        // Cache miss or expired - fetch from a node\n98\t        let client = Client::builder()\n99\t            .timeout(Duration::from_secs(2))\n100\t            .build()\n101\t            .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n102\t\n103\t        for address in &self.node_addresses {\n104\t ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n128\t// Local Rate Limiter (for single-pod deployments)\n129\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n130\t\n131\t/// In-memory rate limiter for admin login (local backend only).\n132\t/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n133\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n134\tpub struct LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n135\t    inner: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<LocalAdminRateLimiterInner>>,\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n139\tstruct LocalAdminRateLimiterInner {\n140\t    /// Map of IP -> (request_timestamps_ms, failed_count, backoff_until_ms)\n141\t    state: HashMap<String, LocalRateLimitState>,\n142\t}\n143\t\n144\t#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\n145\tstruct LocalRateLimitState {\n146\t    /// Timestamps of recent requests (for sliding window)\n147\t    request_timestamps_ms: Vec<i64>,\n148\t    /// Consecutive failed login attempts\n149\t    failed_count: u32,\n150\t    /// Unix timestamp (ms) when backoff expires\n151\t    backoff_until_ms: Option<i64>,\n152\t}\n153\t\n154\timpl LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n155\t    pub fn new() -> Self {\n156\t        Self {\n157\t            inner: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalAdminRateLimiterInner::default())),\n158\t        }\n159\t    }\n160\t\n161\t    /// Check rate limit and exponential backoff.\n162\t    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n163\t    pub fn check(\n164\t        &self,\n165\t        ip: &str,\n166\t        limit: u64,\n167\t        window_ms: u64,\n168\t        failed_threshold: u32,\n169\t        backoff_start_minutes: u64,\n170\t        backoff_max_hours: u64,\n171\t    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n172\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n173\t        let now = now_ms();\n174\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n175\t\n176\t        // Check if we're in backoff mode\n177\t        if let Some(backoff_until) = state.backoff_until_ms {\n178\t            if backoff_until > now {\n179\t                let wait_seconds = ((backoff_until - now) / 1000) as u64;\n180\t                return (false, Some(wait_seconds));\n181\t            }\n182\t            // Backoff expired, clear it\n183\t            state.backoff_until_ms = None;\n184\t        }\n185\t\n186\t        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n187\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n188\t\n189\t        // Check if limit exceeded\n190\t        if state.request_timestamps_ms.len() >= limit as usize {\n191\t            // Enter backoff mode after threshold consecutive failures\n192\t            let failed = state.failed_count + 1;\n193\t            state.failed_count = failed;\n194\t\n195\t            if failed >= failed_threshold {\n196\t                let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((failed - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7)); // Cap at 2^7 = 128x\n197\t                let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n198\t                state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n199\t                return (false, Some(backoff_seconds));\n200\t            }\n201\t\n202\t            return (false, None);\n203\t        }\n204\t\n205\t        // Record this request\n206\t        state.request_timestamps_ms.push(now);\n207\t        (true, None)\n208\t    }\n209\t\n210\t    /// Reset rate limit and backoff state on successful login.\n211\t    pub fn reset(&self, ip: &str) {\n212\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n213\t        inner.state.remove(ip);\n214\t    }\n215\t\n216\t    /// Record a failed login attempt (for backoff calculation).\n217\t    pub fn record_failure(&self, ip: &str, failed_threshold: u32, backoff_start_minutes: u64, backoff_max_hours: u64) -> Option<u64> {\n218\t        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n219\t        let now = now_ms();\n220\t        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n221\t\n222\t        state.failed_count += 1;\n223\t\n224\t        if state.failed_count >= failed_threshold {\n225\t            let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((state.failed_count - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7));\n226\t            let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n227\t            state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n228\t            return Some(backoff_seconds);\n229\t        }\n230\t\n231\t        None\n232\t    }\n233\t}\n234\t\n235\timpl Default for LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n236\t    fn default() -> Self {\n237\t        Self::new()\n238\t    }\n239\t}\n240\t\n241\t/// In-memory rate limiter for search UI (local backend only).\n242\t/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n243\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n244\tpub struct FromRef<S>,\n480\t{\n481\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n482\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n483\t\n484\t    // Count degraded nodes\n485\t    let degraded_count =     error: None,     // TODO: populate from last health check error\n505\t        })\n506\t        .collect();\n507\t\n508\t    // Check if fully covered\n509\t    let fully_covered = degraded_count FromRef<S>,\n528\t{\n529\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n530\t    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n531\t    let mut shards = HashMap::new();\n532\t\n533\t    // Build shard -> node mapping using rendezvous hash\n534\t    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n535\t        let mut node_ids = Vec::new();\n536\t\n537\t        // Collect nodes from all replica groups for this shard\n538\t        for group in topo.groups() {\n539\t            let assigned = router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf());\n540\t            for node_id in assigned {\n541\t                node_ids.push(node_id.as_str().to_string());\n542\t            }\n543\t        }\n544\t\n545\t        shards.insert(shard_id.to_string(), node_ids);\n546\t    }\n547\t\n548\t    Ok(Json(ShardsResponse { shards }))\n549\t}\n550\t\n551\t/// GET /_miroir/ready — readiness probe (503 during startup, 200 once ready).\n552\tpub async fn get_ready<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<&'static str, StatusCode>\n553\twhere\n554\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n555\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n556\t{\n557\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n558\t    let ready = *state.ready.read().await;\n559\t\n560\t    if ready {\n561\t        Ok(\"\")\n562\t    } else {\n563\t        // Not yet marked ready - check if covering quorum exists\n564\t        let has_quorum = state.check_covering_quorum().await;\n565\t        if has_quorum {\n566\t            // Auto-mark ready on first successful quorum check\n567\t            state.mark_ready().await;\n568\t            Ok(\"\")\n569\t        } else {\n570\t            Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n571\t        }\n572\t    }\n573\t}\n574\t\n575\t/// GET /_miroir/metrics — admin-key-gated Prometheus metrics.\n576\tpub async fn get_metrics<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Response\n577\twhere\n578\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n579\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n580\t{\n581\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n582\t    match state.metrics.encode_metrics() {\n583\t        Ok(metrics) => metrics.into_response(),\n584\t        Err(e) => {\n585\t            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n586\t            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()\n587\t        }\n588\t    }\n589\t}\n590\t\n591\t/// POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/rotate-scoped-key — manual rotation trigger.\n592\t///\n593\t/// Admin-gated endpoint that initiates a scoped key rotation for the given index.\n594\t/// Set `force: true` in the request body to bypass the timing gate.\n595\tpub async fn rotate_scoped_key_handler<S>(\n596\t    State(state): State<S>,\n597\t    Path(index): Path<String>,\n598\t    Json(body): Json<RotateScopedKeyRequest>,\n599\t) -> Result<Json<RotateScopedKeyResponse>, (StatusCode, String)>\n600\twhere\n601\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n602\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n603\t{\n604\t    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n605\t\n606\t    let redis = app_state.redis_store.clone().ok_or_else(|| {\n607\t        (\n608\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n609\t            \"scoped key rotation requires Redis task store\".into(),\n610\t        )\n611\t    })?;\n612\t\n613\t    if !app_state.config.search_ui.enabled {\n614\t        return Err((\n615\t            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n616\t            \"search_ui is not enabled\".into(),\n617\t        ));\n618\t    }\n619\t\n620\t    let rotation_state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n621\t        config: app_state.config.clone(),\n622\t        redis,\n623\t        pod_id: app_state.pod_id.clone(),\n624\t    };\n625\t\n626\t    info!(\n627\t        index scoped_key_rotation::check_and_rotate(&rotation_state, &index, body.force).await {\n634\t        Ok(response) => Ok(Json(response)),\n635\t        Err(e) => {\n636\t            error!(index = %index, error = %e, \"manual scoped key rotation failed\");\n637\t            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e))\n638\t        }\n639\t    }\n640\t}\n641\t\n642\t/// Parse a rate limit string like \"10/minute\" into (limit, window_seconds).\n643\tpub fn parse_rate_limit(s: &str) -> Result<(u64, u64), String> {\n644\t    let parts: Vec<&str> = s.split('/').collect();\n645\t    if parts.len() != 2 {\n646\t        return Err(format!(\"invalid rate limit format: '{}', expected 'N/UNIT'\", s));\n647\t    }\n648\t    let limit: u64 = parts[0].parse()\n649\t        .map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid limit number: '{}'\", parts[0]))?;\n650\t    let window_seconds = match parts[1] {\n651\t        \"second\" | \"s\" => 1,\n652\t        \"minute\" | \"m\" => 60,\n653\t        \"hour\" | \"h\" => 3600,\n654\t        \"day\" | \"d\" => 86400,\n655\t        unit => return Err(format!(\"invalid time unit: '{}', expected second/minute/hour/day\", unit)),\n656\t    };\n657\t    Ok((limit, window_seconds))\n658\t}\n659\t\n660\t/// Generate a random session ID.\n661\tfn generate_session_id() -> String {\n662\t    let mut bytes = [0u8; 24];\n663\t    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n664\t    hex::encode(&bytes)\n665\t}\n666\t\n667\t/// POST /_miroir/admin/login — admin login with rate limiting and exponential backoff.\n668\t///\n669\t/// Request body:\n670\t/// ```json\n671\t/// { \"admin_key\": \"...\" }\n672\t/// ```\n673\t///\n674\t/// On success, sets a `miroir_admin_session` cookie and returns:\n675\t/// ```json\n676\t/// { \"success\": true }\n677\t/// ```\n678\t///\n679\t/// Rate limiting (per source IP):\n680\t/// - 10 requests per minute (configurable via `admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip`)\n681\t/// - After 5 consecutive failed attempts, exponential backoff applies:\n682\t///   - 10m, 20m, 40m, ... up to 24h cap\n683\t///\n684\t/// Successful login resets both the rate limit counter and backoff state.\n685\tpub async fn admin_login<S>(\n686\t    State(state): State<S>,\n687\t    headers: HeaderMap,\n688\t    Json(body): Json<AdminLoginRequest>,\n689\t) -> Response\n690\twhere\n691\t    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n692\t    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n693\t{\n694\t    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n695\t\n696\t    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n697\t    let source_ip = headers\n698\t        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n699\t        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n700\t        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n701\t        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n702\t        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n703\t        .trim()\n704\t        .to_string();\n705\t\n706\t    // Parse rate limit config\n707\t    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n708\t        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n709\t        Err(e) => {\n710\t            error!(error = %e, \"invalid admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config\");\n711\t            return (\n712\t                StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n713\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n714\t                    success: false,\n715\t                    message: Some(\"Rate limit configuration error\".into()),\n716\t                }),\n717\t            ).into_response();\n718\t        }\n719\t    };\n720\t\n721\t    // Check rate limit and backoff\n722\t    let backend = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n723\t    if backend == \"redis\" {\n724\t        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n725\t            match redis.check_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n726\t                Ok((allowed, wait_seconds)) => {\n727\t                    if !allowed {\n728\t                        if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n729\t                            warn!(\n730\t                                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n731\t                                wait_seconds = ws,\n732\t                                \"admin login rate limited (backoff)\"\n733\t                            );\n734\t                            return (\n735\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n736\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n737\t                                    success: false,\n738\t                                    message: Some(format!(\n739\t                                        \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n740\t                                        ws\n741\t                                    )),\n742\t                                }),\n743\t                            ).into_response();\n744\t                        } else {\n745\t                            return (\n746\t                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n747\t                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n748\t                                    success: false,\n749\t                                    message: Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into()),\n750\t                                }),\n751\t                            ).into_response();\n752\t                        }\n753\t                    }\n754\t                    // Allowed, proceed\n755\t                }\n756\t                Err(e) => {\n757\t                    error!(error = %e, \"failed to check admin login rate limit\");\n758\t                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n759\t                }\n760\t            }\n761\t        }\n762\t    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n763\t        // Local backend rate limiting\n764\t        let (allowed, wait_seconds) = state.local_rate_limiter.check(\n765\t            &source_ip,\n766\t            limit,\n767\t            window_seconds * 1000,\n768\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold,\n769\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes,\n770\t            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours * 60,\n771\t        );\n772\t        if !allowed {\n773\t            warn!(\n774\t                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n775\t                wait_seconds = ?wait_seconds,\n776\t                \"admin login rate limited (local backend)\"\n777\t            );\n778\t            return (\n779\t                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n780\t                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n781\t                    success: false,\n782\t                    message: if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n783\t                        Some(format!(\n784\t                            \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n785\t                            ws\n786\t                        ))\n787\t                    } else {\n788\t                        Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into())\n789\t                    },\n790\t                }),\n791\t            ).into_response();\n792\t        }\n793\t    }\n794\t\n795\t    // Verify admin_key (constant-time comparison to prevent timing side-channels)\n796\t    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n797\t    if body.admin_key.as_bytes().ct_eq(state.config.admin.api_key.as_bytes()).into() {\n798\t        // Successful login - reset rate limit counters\n799\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n800\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n801\t                if let Err(e) = redis.reset_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip) {\n802\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to reset admin login rate limit\");\n803\t                }\n804\t            }\n805\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n806\t            state.local_rate_limiter.reset(&source_ip);\n807\t        }\n808\t\n809\t        // Generate session ID and seal it\n810\t        let session_id = generate_session_id();\n811\t        let sealed = match seal_session(&session_id, &state.seal_key) {\n812\t            Ok(sealed) => sealed,\n813\t            Err(e) => {\n814\t                error!(error = %e, \"failed to seal admin session\");\n815\t                return (\n816\t                    StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n817\t                    Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n818\t                        success: false,\n819\t                        message: Some(\"Failed to create session\".into()),\n820\t                    }),\n821\t                ).into_response();\n822\t            }\n823\t        };\n824\t\n825\t        info!(\n826\t            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n827\t            session_prefix = &session_id[..8],\n828\t            \"admin login successful\"\n829\t        );\n830\t\n831\t        // Set cookie and return success\n832\t        (\n833\t            StatusCode::OK,\n834\t            [\n835\t                (\"Set-Cookie\", format!(\"{}={}; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/; Max-Age={}\",\n836\t                    COOKIE_NAME, sealed, state.config.admin_ui.session_ttl_s)),\n837\t            ],\n838\t            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n839\t                success: true,\n840\t                message: None,\n841\t            }),\n842\t        ).into_response()\n843\t    } else {\n844\t        // Wrong admin_key - record failure for backoff tracking\n845\t        warn!(\n846\t            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n847\t            \"admin login failed: invalid admin_key\"\n848\t        );\n849\t\n850\t        if backend == \"redis\" {\n851\t            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n852\t                let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n853\t                let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n854\t                let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n855\t\n856\t                if let Err(e) = redis.record_failure_admin_login(\n857\t                    &source_ip,\n858\t                    failed_threshold,\n859\t                    backoff_start_minutes,\n860\t                    backoff_max_hours,\n861\t                ) {\n862\t                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to record admin login failure\");\n863\t                }\n864\t            }\n865\t        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n866\t            let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n867\t            let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n868\t            let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n869\t\n870\t            state.local_rate_limiter.record_failure(\n871\t                &source_ip,\n872\t                failed_threshold,\n873\t                backoff_start_minutes,\n874\t                backoff_max_hours * 60,\n875\t            );\n876\t        }\n877\t\n878\t        (\n879\t            StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED,\n880\t            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n881\t                success: false,\n882\t                message: Some(\"Invalid admin key\".into()),\n883\t            }),\n884\t        ).into_response()\n885\t    }\n886\t}\n887\t\n888\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n889\t// Rebalancer Admin API Endpoints (plan §4)\n890\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n891\t\n892\t/// POST /_miroir/nodes — Add a node to a replica group.\n893\t///\n894\t/// Request body:\n895\t/// scoped_key_rotation::{self, ScopedKeyRotationState, RotateScopedKeyRequest, RotateScopedKeyResponse},\n};\n\n/// Hash a PII value (IP address) for safe log correlation.\nfn hash_for_log(value: &str) -> String {\n    use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n    let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();\n    value.hash(&mut hasher);\n    format!(\"{:016x}\", hasher.finish())\n}\n\n/// Request body for POST /_miroir/admin/login.\n#[derive(Deserialize)]\npub struct AdminLoginRequest {\n    pub admin_key: String,\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Debug for AdminLoginRequest {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        f.debug_struct(\"AdminLoginRequest\")\n            .field(\"admin_key\", &\"[redacted]\")\n            .finish()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Response body for POST /_miroir/admin/login.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct AdminLoginResponse {\n    pub success: bool,\n    pub message: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Version state with cache for fetching Meilisearch version.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct VersionState {\n    pub node_master_key: String,\n    pub node_addresses: Vec<String>,\n    pub version_cache: Arc<RwLock<Option<String>>>,\n    pub last_cache_update: Arc<RwLock<Option<std::time::Instant>>>,\n    pub cache_ttl_secs: u64,\n}\n\nimpl VersionState {\n    pub fn new(node_master_key: String, node_addresses: Vec<String>) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            node_master_key,\n            node_addresses,\n            version_cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(None)),\n            last_cache_update: Arc::new(RwLock::new(None)),\n            cache_ttl_secs: 60,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Fetch version from a healthy node, using cache if within TTL.\n    pub async fn get_version(&self) -> Result<String, StatusCode> {\n        // Check cache first\n        {\n            let cache = self.version_cache.read().await;\n            let last_update = self.last_cache_update.read().await;\n            if let (Some(ref cached), Some(last)) = (cache.as_ref(), last_update.as_ref()) {\n                if last.elapsed().as_secs() < self.cache_ttl_secs {\n                    return Ok((**cached).clone());\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Cache miss or expired - fetch from a node\n        let client = Client::builder()\n            .timeout(Duration::from_secs(2))\n            .build()\n            .map_err(|_| StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR)?;\n\n        for address in &self.node_addresses {\n            let url = format!(\"{}/version\", address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n            let response = client\n                .get(&url)\n                .header(\"Authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key))\n                .send()\n                .await;\n\n            if let Ok(resp) = response {\n                if resp.status().is_success() {\n                    if let Ok(body) = resp.text().await {\n                        // Update cache\n                        *self.version_cache.write().await = Some(body.clone());\n                        *self.last_cache_update.write().await = Some(std::time::Instant::now());\n                        return Ok(body);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Local Rate Limiter (for single-pod deployments)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// In-memory rate limiter for admin login (local backend only).\n/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n    inner: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<LocalAdminRateLimiterInner>>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct LocalAdminRateLimiterInner {\n    /// Map of IP -> (request_timestamps_ms, failed_count, backoff_until_ms)\n    state: HashMap<String, LocalRateLimitState>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Default, Clone)]\nstruct LocalRateLimitState {\n    /// Timestamps of recent requests (for sliding window)\n    request_timestamps_ms: Vec<i64>,\n    /// Consecutive failed login attempts\n    failed_count: u32,\n    /// Unix timestamp (ms) when backoff expires\n    backoff_until_ms: Option<i64>,\n}\n\nimpl LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            inner: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalAdminRateLimiterInner::default())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check rate limit and exponential backoff.\n    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n    pub fn check(\n        &self,\n        ip: &str,\n        limit: u64,\n        window_ms: u64,\n        failed_threshold: u32,\n        backoff_start_minutes: u64,\n        backoff_max_hours: u64,\n    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n\n        // Check if we're in backoff mode\n        if let Some(backoff_until) = state.backoff_until_ms {\n            if backoff_until > now {\n                let wait_seconds = ((backoff_until - now) / 1000) as u64;\n                return (false, Some(wait_seconds));\n            }\n            // Backoff expired, clear it\n            state.backoff_until_ms = None;\n        }\n\n        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n        state.request_timestamps_ms.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n\n        // Check if limit exceeded\n        if state.request_timestamps_ms.len() >= limit as usize {\n            // Enter backoff mode after threshold consecutive failures\n            let failed = state.failed_count + 1;\n            state.failed_count = failed;\n\n            if failed >= failed_threshold {\n                let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((failed - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7)); // Cap at 2^7 = 128x\n                let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n                state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n                return (false, Some(backoff_seconds));\n            }\n\n            return (false, None);\n        }\n\n        // Record this request\n        state.request_timestamps_ms.push(now);\n        (true, None)\n    }\n\n    /// Reset rate limit and backoff state on successful login.\n    pub fn reset(&self, ip: &str) {\n        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n        inner.state.remove(ip);\n    }\n\n    /// Record a failed login attempt (for backoff calculation).\n    pub fn record_failure(&self, ip: &str, failed_threshold: u32, backoff_start_minutes: u64, backoff_max_hours: u64) -> Option<u64> {\n        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let state = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n\n        state.failed_count += 1;\n\n        if state.failed_count >= failed_threshold {\n            let backoff_minutes = backoff_start_minutes * (1u64 << ((state.failed_count - failed_threshold) as u64).min(7));\n            let backoff_seconds = (backoff_minutes * 60).min(backoff_max_hours * 3600);\n            state.backoff_until_ms = Some(now + (backoff_seconds as i64 * 1000));\n            return Some(backoff_seconds);\n        }\n\n        None\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for LocalAdminRateLimiter {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// In-memory rate limiter for search UI (local backend only).\n/// Thread-safe using Arc<Mutex<...>>.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n    inner: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner>>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner {\n    /// Map of IP -> request_timestamps_ms\n    state: HashMap<String, Vec<i64>>,\n}\n\nimpl LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            inner: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(LocalSearchUiRateLimiterInner::default())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check rate limit for search UI.\n    /// Returns (allowed, wait_seconds).\n    pub fn check(\n        &self,\n        ip: &str,\n        limit: u64,\n        window_ms: u64,\n    ) -> (bool, Option<u64>) {\n        let mut inner = self.inner.lock().unwrap();\n        let now = now_ms();\n        let timestamps = inner.state.entry(ip.to_string()).or_default();\n\n        // Clean old timestamps outside the window\n        timestamps.retain(|&ts| now - ts < window_ms as i64);\n\n        // Check if limit exceeded\n        if timestamps.len() >= limit as usize {\n            return (false, None);\n        }\n\n        // Record this request\n        timestamps.push(now);\n        (true, None)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for LocalSearchUiRateLimiter {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get current time in milliseconds since Unix epoch.\nfn now_ms() -> i64 {\n    std::time::SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .unwrap_or_default()\n        .as_millis() as i64\n}\n\n/// Shared application state for admin endpoints.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AppState {\n    pub config: Arc<MiroirConfig>,\n    pub topology: Arc<RwLock<Topology>>,\n    pub ready: Arc<RwLock<bool>>,\n    pub metrics: super::super::middleware::Metrics,\n    pub version_state: VersionState,\n    pub task_registry: Arc<TaskRegistryImpl>,\n    pub redis_store: Option<RedisTaskStore>,\n    pub pod_id: String,\n    pub seal_key: SealKey,\n    pub local_rate_limiter: LocalAdminRateLimiter,\n    pub local_search_ui_rate_limiter: LocalSearchUiRateLimiter,\n    pub rebalancer: Option<Arc<Rebalancer>>,\n    pub migration_coordinator: Option<Arc<RwLock<MigrationCoordinator>>>,\n}\n\nimpl AppState {\n    pub fn new(\n        config: MiroirConfig,\n        metrics: super::super::middleware::Metrics,\n        seal_key: SealKey,\n    ) -> Self {\n        Self::with_redis(config, metrics, None, \"unknown\".into(), seal_key)\n    }\n\n    pub fn with_redis(\n        config: MiroirConfig,\n        metrics: super::super::middleware::Metrics,\n        redis_store: Option<RedisTaskStore>,\n        pod_id: String,\n        seal_key: SealKey,\n    ) -> Self {\n        // Build initial topology from config\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(\n            config.shards,\n            config.replica_groups,\n            config.replication_factor as usize,\n        );\n\n        for node_config in &config.nodes {\n            let node = Node::new(\n                NodeId::new(node_config.id.clone()),\n                node_config.address.clone(),\n                node_config.replica_group,\n            );\n            // Start nodes in Joining state - health checker will promote to Active\n            topology.add_node(node);\n        }\n\n        let version_state = VersionState::new(\n            config.node_master_key.clone(),\n            config.nodes.iter().map(|n| n.address.clone()).collect(),\n        );\n\n        // Select task registry backend based on config\n        let task_registry = match config.task_store.backend.as_str() {\n            \"redis\" if redis_store.is_some() => {\n                let store = redis_store.as_ref().unwrap().clone();\n                store.migrate().expect(\"Redis migration failed\");\n                TaskRegistryImpl::Redis(Arc::new(store))\n            }\n            \"sqlite\" if !config.task_store.path.is_empty() => {\n                TaskRegistryImpl::sqlite(&config.task_store.path)\n                    .expect(\"Failed to open SQLite task store\")\n            }\n            _ => TaskRegistryImpl::in_memory(),\n        };\n\n        let topology_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(topology));\n\n        // Initialize rebalancer and migration coordinator\n        let rebalancer_config = RebalancerConfig {\n            max_concurrent_migrations: config.rebalancer.max_concurrent_migrations,\n            migration_timeout_s: config.rebalancer.migration_timeout_s,\n            auto_rebalance_on_recovery: config.rebalancer.auto_rebalance_on_recovery,\n            migration_batch_size: 1000,\n            migration_batch_delay_ms: 100,\n        };\n\n        let migration_config = MigrationConfig {\n            drain_timeout: std::time::Duration::from_secs(30),\n            skip_delta_pass: false,\n            anti_entropy_enabled: config.anti_entropy.enabled,\n        };\n\n        let migration_coordinator = Arc::new(RwLock::new(\n            MigrationCoordinator::new(migration_config.clone())\n        ));\n\n        // Create migration executor for actual HTTP document migration\n        use miroir_core::rebalancer::HttpMigrationExecutor;\n        let migration_executor = Arc::new(HttpMigrationExecutor::new(\n            config.node_master_key.clone(),\n            config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n        ));\n\n        let rebalancer = Arc::new(Rebalancer::new(\n            rebalancer_config,\n            topology_arc.clone(),\n            migration_config,\n        ).with_migration_executor(migration_executor));\n\n        Self {\n            config: Arc::new(config),\n            topology: topology_arc,\n            ready: Arc::new(RwLock::new(false)),\n            metrics,\n            version_state,\n            task_registry: Arc::new(task_registry),\n            redis_store,\n            pod_id,\n            seal_key,\n            local_rate_limiter: LocalAdminRateLimiter::new(),\n            local_search_ui_rate_limiter: LocalSearchUiRateLimiter::new(),\n            rebalancer: Some(rebalancer),\n            migration_coordinator: Some(migration_coordinator),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Mark the service as ready (all nodes reachable).\n    pub async fn mark_ready(&self) {\n        *self.ready.write().await = true;\n        info!(\"Service marked as ready\");\n    }\n\n    /// Check if a covering quorum is reachable.\n    pub async fn check_covering_quorum(&self) -> bool {\n        let topo = self.topology.read().await;\n        let node_map = topo.node_map();\n\n        // For each replica group, check if we have enough healthy nodes\n        for group in topo.groups() {\n            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n            let required = (topo.rf() + 1) / 2; // Simple majority for quorum\n            if healthy.len() < required {\n                return false;\n            }\n        }\n\n        true\n    }\n}\n\n/// Response for GET /_miroir/topology (plan §10 JSON shape).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TopologyResponse {\n    pub shards: u32,\n    pub replication_factor: u32,\n    pub nodes: Vec<NodeInfo>,\n    pub degraded_node_count: u32,\n    pub rebalance_in_progress: bool,\n    pub fully_covered: bool,\n}\n\n/// Per-node information in the topology response.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct NodeInfo {\n    pub id: String,\n    pub address: String,\n    pub status: String,\n    pub shard_count: u32,\n    pub last_seen_ms: u64,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub error: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Response for GET /_miroir/shards.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct ShardsResponse {\n    pub shards: HashMap<String, Vec<String>>, // shard_id -> list of node IDs\n}\n\n/// GET /_miroir/topology — full cluster state per plan §10.\npub async fn get_topology<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<TopologyResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n\n    // Count degraded nodes\n    let degraded_count = topo.nodes().filter(|n| !n.is_healthy()).count() as u32;\n\n    // Check rebalance status\n    let rebalance_in_progress = if let Some(ref rebalancer) = state.rebalancer {\n        let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n        status.in_progress\n    } else {\n        false\n    };\n\n    // Build node info list\n    let nodes: Vec<NodeInfo> = topo\n        .nodes()\n        .map(|n| NodeInfo {\n            id: n.id.as_str().to_string(),\n            address: n.address.clone(),\n            status: format!(\"{:?}\", n.status).to_lowercase(),\n            shard_count: 0, // TODO: compute from routing table\n            last_seen_ms: 0, // TODO: track last health check time\n            error: None,     // TODO: populate from last health check error\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Check if fully covered\n    let fully_covered = degraded_count == 0;\n\n    let response = TopologyResponse {\n        shards: topo.shards,\n        replication_factor: topo.rf() as u32,\n        nodes,\n        degraded_node_count: degraded_count,\n        rebalance_in_progress,\n        fully_covered,\n    };\n\n    Ok(Json(response))\n}\n\n/// GET /_miroir/shards — shard → node mapping table.\npub async fn get_shards<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<Json<ShardsResponse>, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let mut shards = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Build shard -> node mapping using rendezvous hash\n    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n        let mut node_ids = Vec::new();\n\n        // Collect nodes from all replica groups for this shard\n        for group in topo.groups() {\n            let assigned = router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf());\n            for node_id in assigned {\n                node_ids.push(node_id.as_str().to_string());\n            }\n        }\n\n        shards.insert(shard_id.to_string(), node_ids);\n    }\n\n    Ok(Json(ShardsResponse { shards }))\n}\n\n/// GET /_miroir/ready — readiness probe (503 during startup, 200 once ready).\npub async fn get_ready<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Result<&'static str, StatusCode>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n    let ready = *state.ready.read().await;\n\n    if ready {\n        Ok(\"\")\n    } else {\n        // Not yet marked ready - check if covering quorum exists\n        let has_quorum = state.check_covering_quorum().await;\n        if has_quorum {\n            // Auto-mark ready on first successful quorum check\n            state.mark_ready().await;\n            Ok(\"\")\n        } else {\n            Err(StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE)\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// GET /_miroir/metrics — admin-key-gated Prometheus metrics.\npub async fn get_metrics<S>(State(state): State<S>) -> Response\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n    match state.metrics.encode_metrics() {\n        Ok(metrics) => metrics.into_response(),\n        Err(e) => {\n            tracing::error!(error = %e, \"failed to encode metrics\");\n            StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.into_response()\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// POST /_miroir/ui/search/{index}/rotate-scoped-key — manual rotation trigger.\n///\n/// Admin-gated endpoint that initiates a scoped key rotation for the given index.\n/// Set `force: true` in the request body to bypass the timing gate.\npub async fn rotate_scoped_key_handler<S>(\n    State(state): State<S>,\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Json(body): Json<RotateScopedKeyRequest>,\n) -> Result<Json<RotateScopedKeyResponse>, (StatusCode, String)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    let redis = app_state.redis_store.clone().ok_or_else(|| {\n        (\n            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n            \"scoped key rotation requires Redis task store\".into(),\n        )\n    })?;\n\n    if !app_state.config.search_ui.enabled {\n        return Err((\n            StatusCode::PRECONDITION_FAILED,\n            \"search_ui is not enabled\".into(),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    let rotation_state = ScopedKeyRotationState {\n        config: app_state.config.clone(),\n        redis,\n        pod_id: app_state.pod_id.clone(),\n    };\n\n    info!(\n        index = %index,\n        force = body.force,\n        pod_id = %app_state.pod_id,\n        \"manual scoped key rotation triggered\"\n    );\n\n    match scoped_key_rotation::check_and_rotate(&rotation_state, &index, body.force).await {\n        Ok(response) => Ok(Json(response)),\n        Err(e) => {\n            error!(index = %index, error = %e, \"manual scoped key rotation failed\");\n            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Parse a rate limit string like \"10/minute\" into (limit, window_seconds).\npub fn parse_rate_limit(s: &str) -> Result<(u64, u64), String> {\n    let parts: Vec<&str> = s.split('/').collect();\n    if parts.len() != 2 {\n        return Err(format!(\"invalid rate limit format: '{}', expected 'N/UNIT'\", s));\n    }\n    let limit: u64 = parts[0].parse()\n        .map_err(|_| format!(\"invalid limit number: '{}'\", parts[0]))?;\n    let window_seconds = match parts[1] {\n        \"second\" | \"s\" => 1,\n        \"minute\" | \"m\" => 60,\n        \"hour\" | \"h\" => 3600,\n        \"day\" | \"d\" => 86400,\n        unit => return Err(format!(\"invalid time unit: '{}', expected second/minute/hour/day\", unit)),\n    };\n    Ok((limit, window_seconds))\n}\n\n/// Generate a random session ID.\nfn generate_session_id() -> String {\n    let mut bytes = [0u8; 24];\n    rand::rngs::OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut bytes);\n    hex::encode(&bytes)\n}\n\n/// POST /_miroir/admin/login — admin login with rate limiting and exponential backoff.\n///\n/// Request body:\n/// ```json\n/// { \"admin_key\": \"...\" }\n/// ```\n///\n/// On success, sets a `miroir_admin_session` cookie and returns:\n/// ```json\n/// { \"success\": true }\n/// ```\n///\n/// Rate limiting (per source IP):\n/// - 10 requests per minute (configurable via `admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip`)\n/// - After 5 consecutive failed attempts, exponential backoff applies:\n///   - 10m, 20m, 40m, ... up to 24h cap\n///\n/// Successful login resets both the rate limit counter and backoff state.\npub async fn admin_login<S>(\n    State(state): State<S>,\n    headers: HeaderMap,\n    Json(body): Json<AdminLoginRequest>,\n) -> Response\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    // Extract source IP from X-Forwarded-For or X-Real-IP (trust proxy)\n    let source_ip = headers\n        .get(\"x-forwarded-for\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())\n        .and_then(|s| s.split(',').next())\n        .or_else(|| headers.get(\"x-real-ip\").and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok()))\n        .unwrap_or(\"unknown\")\n        .trim()\n        .to_string();\n\n    // Parse rate limit config\n    let (limit, window_seconds) = match parse_rate_limit(&state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip) {\n        Ok(parsed) => parsed,\n        Err(e) => {\n            error!(error = %e, \"invalid admin_ui.rate_limit.per_ip config\");\n            return (\n                StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n                    success: false,\n                    message: Some(\"Rate limit configuration error\".into()),\n                }),\n            ).into_response();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Check rate limit and backoff\n    let backend = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backend.as_str();\n    if backend == \"redis\" {\n        if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n            match redis.check_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip, limit, window_seconds) {\n                Ok((allowed, wait_seconds)) => {\n                    if !allowed {\n                        if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n                            warn!(\n                                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                                wait_seconds = ws,\n                                \"admin login rate limited (backoff)\"\n                            );\n                            return (\n                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n                                    success: false,\n                                    message: Some(format!(\n                                        \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n                                        ws\n                                    )),\n                                }),\n                            ).into_response();\n                        } else {\n                            return (\n                                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n                                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n                                    success: false,\n                                    message: Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into()),\n                                }),\n                            ).into_response();\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Allowed, proceed\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    error!(error = %e, \"failed to check admin login rate limit\");\n                    // Continue anyway on error (fail-open)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    } else if backend == \"local\" {\n        // Local backend rate limiting\n        let (allowed, wait_seconds) = state.local_rate_limiter.check(\n            &source_ip,\n            limit,\n            window_seconds * 1000,\n            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold,\n            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes,\n            state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours * 60,\n        );\n        if !allowed {\n            warn!(\n                source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n                wait_seconds = ?wait_seconds,\n                \"admin login rate limited (local backend)\"\n            );\n            return (\n                StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS,\n                Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n                    success: false,\n                    message: if let Some(ws) = wait_seconds {\n                        Some(format!(\n                            \"Too many failed login attempts. Try again in {} seconds.\",\n                            ws\n                        ))\n                    } else {\n                        Some(\"Too many login attempts. Please try again later.\".into())\n                    },\n                }),\n            ).into_response();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify admin_key (constant-time comparison to prevent timing side-channels)\n    use subtle::ConstantTimeEq as _;\n    if body.admin_key.as_bytes().ct_eq(state.config.admin.api_key.as_bytes()).into() {\n        // Successful login - reset rate limit counters\n        if backend == \"redis\" {\n            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n                if let Err(e) = redis.reset_rate_limit_admin_login(&source_ip) {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to reset admin login rate limit\");\n                }\n            }\n        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n            state.local_rate_limiter.reset(&source_ip);\n        }\n\n        // Generate session ID and seal it\n        let session_id = generate_session_id();\n        let sealed = match seal_session(&session_id, &state.seal_key) {\n            Ok(sealed) => sealed,\n            Err(e) => {\n                error!(error = %e, \"failed to seal admin session\");\n                return (\n                    StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n                    Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n                        success: false,\n                        message: Some(\"Failed to create session\".into()),\n                    }),\n                ).into_response();\n            }\n        };\n\n        info!(\n            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n            session_prefix = &session_id[..8],\n            \"admin login successful\"\n        );\n\n        // Set cookie and return success\n        (\n            StatusCode::OK,\n            [\n                (\"Set-Cookie\", format!(\"{}={}; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/; Max-Age={}\",\n                    COOKIE_NAME, sealed, state.config.admin_ui.session_ttl_s)),\n            ],\n            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n                success: true,\n                message: None,\n            }),\n        ).into_response()\n    } else {\n        // Wrong admin_key - record failure for backoff tracking\n        warn!(\n            source_ip_hash = hash_for_log(&source_ip),\n            \"admin login failed: invalid admin_key\"\n        );\n\n        if backend == \"redis\" {\n            if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n                let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n                let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n                let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n\n                if let Err(e) = redis.record_failure_admin_login(\n                    &source_ip,\n                    failed_threshold,\n                    backoff_start_minutes,\n                    backoff_max_hours,\n                ) {\n                    warn!(error = %e, \"failed to record admin login failure\");\n                }\n            }\n        } else if backend == \"local\" {\n            let backoff_start_minutes = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_start_minutes;\n            let backoff_max_hours = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.backoff_max_hours;\n            let failed_threshold = state.config.admin_ui.rate_limit.failed_attempt_threshold;\n\n            state.local_rate_limiter.record_failure(\n                &source_ip,\n                failed_threshold,\n                backoff_start_minutes,\n                backoff_max_hours * 60,\n            );\n        }\n\n        (\n            StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED,\n            Json(AdminLoginResponse {\n                success: false,\n                message: Some(\"Invalid admin key\".into()),\n            }),\n        ).into_response()\n    }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Rebalancer Admin API Endpoints (plan §4)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/// POST /_miroir/nodes — Add a node to a replica group.\n///\n/// Request body:\n/// ```json\n/// {\n///   \"id\": \"node-new\",\n///   \"address\": \"http://node-new:7700\",\n///   \"replica_group\": 0\n/// }\n/// ```\npub async fn add_node<S>(\n    State(state): State<S>,\n    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    let rebalancer = app_state.rebalancer.as_ref()\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, \"Rebalancer not initialized\".into()))?;\n\n    let id = body.get(\"id\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'id' field\".into()))?\n        .to_string();\n\n    let address = body.get(\"address\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'address' field\".into()))?\n        .to_string();\n\n    let replica_group = body.get(\"replica_group\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'replica_group' field\".into()))?\n        as u32;\n\n    use miroir_core::rebalancer::AddNodeRequest;\n    let request = AddNodeRequest { id: id.clone(), address, replica_group };\n\n    match rebalancer.add_node(request).await {\n        Ok(result) => {\n            info!(node_id = %id, replica_group, \"Node addition started\");\n            Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n                \"operation_id\": result.id,\n                \"message\": result.message,\n                \"migrations_count\": result.migrations_count,\n            })))\n        }\n        Err(e) => {\n            error!(error = %e, node_id = %id, \"Node addition failed\");\n            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// DELETE /_miroir/nodes/{id} — Remove a node from the cluster.\npub async fn remove_node<S>(\n    State(state): State<S>,\n    Path(node_id): Path<String>,\n    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    let rebalancer = app_state.rebalancer.as_ref()\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, \"Rebalancer not initialized\".into()))?;\n\n    let force = body.get(\"force\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_bool())\n        .unwrap_or(false);\n\n    use miroir_core::rebalancer::RemoveNodeRequest;\n    let request = RemoveNodeRequest { node_id: node_id.clone(), force };\n\n    match rebalancer.remove_node(request).await {\n        Ok(result) => {\n            info!(node_id = %node_id, \"Node removal completed\");\n            Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n                \"operation_id\": result.id,\n                \"message\": result.message,\n            })))\n        }\n        Err(e) => {\n            error!(error = %e, node_id = %node_id, \"Node removal failed\");\n            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// POST /_miroir/nodes/{id}/drain — Drain a node (prepare for removal).\npub async fn drain_node<S>(\n    State(state): State<S>,\n    Path(node_id): Path<String>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    let rebalancer = app_state.rebalancer.as_ref()\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, \"Rebalancer not initialized\".into()))?;\n\n    use miroir_core::rebalancer::DrainNodeRequest;\n    let request = DrainNodeRequest { node_id: node_id.clone() };\n\n    match rebalancer.drain_node(request).await {\n        Ok(result) => {\n            info!(node_id = %node_id, migrations = result.migrations_count, \"Node drain started\");\n            Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n                \"operation_id\": result.id,\n                \"message\": result.message,\n                \"migrations_count\": result.migrations_count,\n            })))\n        }\n        Err(e) => {\n            error!(error = %e, node_id = %node_id, \"Node drain failed\");\n            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// GET /_miroir/rebalance/status — Get current rebalance status.\npub async fn get_rebalance_status<S>(\n    State(state): State<S>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    let rebalancer = app_state.rebalancer.as_ref()\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, \"Rebalancer not initialized\".into()))?;\n\n    let status = rebalancer.status().await;\n    let metrics = rebalancer.metrics.read().await;\n\n    Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n        \"in_progress\": status.in_progress,\n        \"operations\": status.operations,\n        \"migrations\": status.migrations,\n        \"metrics\": {\n            \"documents_migrated_total\": metrics.documents_migrated_total,\n            \"active_migrations\": metrics.active_migrations,\n            \"current_duration_secs\": metrics.current_duration_secs(),\n        },\n    })))\n}\n\n/// POST /_miroir/replica_groups — Add a replica group.\n///\n/// Request body:\n/// ```json\n/// {\n///   \"group_id\": 2,\n///   \"nodes\": [\n///     {\"id\": \"node-6\", \"address\": \"http://node-6:7700\"},\n///     {\"id\": \"node-7\", \"address\": \"http://node-7:7700\"}\n///   ]\n/// }\n/// ```\npub async fn add_replica_group<S>(\n    State(state): State<S>,\n    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    let rebalancer = app_state.rebalancer.as_ref()\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, \"Rebalancer not initialized\".into()))?;\n\n    let group_id = body.get(\"group_id\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'group_id' field\".into()))?\n        as u32;\n\n    let nodes_array = body.get(\"nodes\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_array())\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing 'nodes' field\".into()))?;\n\n    let mut nodes = Vec::new();\n    for node_obj in nodes_array {\n        let id = node_obj.get(\"id\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing node 'id'\".into()))?\n            .to_string();\n\n        let address = node_obj.get(\"address\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, \"Missing node 'address'\".into()))?\n            .to_string();\n\n        use miroir_core::rebalancer::GroupNodeSpec;\n        nodes.push(GroupNodeSpec { id, address });\n    }\n\n    use miroir_core::rebalancer::AddReplicaGroupRequest;\n    let request = AddReplicaGroupRequest { group_id, nodes };\n\n    match rebalancer.add_replica_group(request).await {\n        Ok(result) => {\n            info!(group_id, \"Replica group addition completed\");\n            Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n                \"operation_id\": result.id,\n                \"message\": result.message,\n            })))\n        }\n        Err(e) => {\n            error!(error = %e, group_id, \"Replica group addition failed\");\n            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// DELETE /_miroir/replica_groups/{id} — Remove a replica group.\npub async fn remove_replica_group<S>(\n    State(state): State<S>,\n    Path(group_id): Path<u32>,\n    Json(body): Json<serde_json::Value>,\n) -> Result<Json<serde_json::Value>, (StatusCode, String)>\nwhere\n    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,\n    AppState: FromRef<S>,\n{\n    let app_state = AppState::from_ref(&state);\n\n    let rebalancer = app_state.rebalancer.as_ref()\n        .ok_or_else(|| (StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, \"Rebalancer not initialized\".into()))?;\n\n    let force = body.get(\"force\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_bool())\n        .unwrap_or(false);\n\n    use miroir_core::rebalancer::RemoveReplicaGroupRequest;\n    let request = RemoveReplicaGroupRequest { group_id, force };\n\n    match rebalancer.remove_replica_group(request).await {\n        Ok(result) => {\n            info!(group_id, \"Replica group removal completed\");\n            Ok(Json(serde_json::json!({\n                \"operation_id\": result.id,\n                \"message\": result.message,\n            })))\n        }\n        Err(e) => {\n            error!(error = %e, group_id, \"Replica group removal failed\");\n            Err((StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_topology_response_serialization() {\n        let response = TopologyResponse {\n            shards: 64,\n            replication_factor: 2,\n            nodes: vec![\n                NodeInfo {\n                    id: \"meili-0\".to_string(),\n                    address: \"http://meili-0.search.svc:7700\".to_string(),\n                    status: \"healthy\".to_string(),\n                    shard_count: 32,\n                    last_seen_ms: 100,\n                    error: None,\n                },\n                NodeInfo {\n                    id: \"meili-1\".to_string(),\n                    address: \"http://meili-1.search.svc:7700\".to_string(),\n                    status: \"degraded\".to_string(),\n                    shard_count: 32,\n                    last_seen_ms: 5000,\n                    error: Some(\"connection refused\".to_string()),\n                },\n            ],\n            degraded_node_count: 1,\n            rebalance_in_progress: false,\n            fully_covered: false,\n        };\n\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(&response).unwrap();\n        assert!(json.contains(\"\\\"shards\\\":64\"));\n        assert!(json.contains(\"\\\"replication_factor\\\":2\"));\n        assert!(json.contains(\"\\\"degraded_node_count\\\":1\"));\n        assert!(json.contains(\"\\\"fully_covered\\\":false\"));\n        assert!(json.contains(\"\\\"status\\\":\\\"healthy\\\"\"));\n        assert!(json.contains(\"\\\"error\\\":\\\"connection refused\\\"\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shards_response_serialization() {\n        let mut shards = HashMap::new();\n        shards.insert(\"0\".to_string(), vec![\"node-0\".to_string(), \"node-1\".to_string()]);\n        shards.insert(\"1\".to_string(), vec![\"node-1\".to_string(), \"node-0\".to_string()]);\n\n        let response = ShardsResponse { shards };\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(&response).unwrap();\n        assert!(json.contains(\"\\\"0\\\"\"));\n        assert!(json.contains(\"\\\"node-0\\\"\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_node_info_with_optional_error() {\n        let info = NodeInfo {\n            id: \"test\".to_string(),\n            address: \"http://meili-0.search.svc:7700\".to_string(),\n            status:
